question_types: Computer-based Case Simulations (CCS) — Step 3 Day 2 only
exam_price_notes: Fee for US/Canadian applicants. International applicants pay additional regional surcharge.

testing_centers.notes: Computer-based testing at Prometric authorized test centers globally

id_requirements: Two forms of ID required. Primary: valid, unexpired government-issued photo ID (passport strongly recommended internationally). Secondary: any ID with name. Name must match USMLE registration exactly.
exam_rules: Closed-book. On-screen calculator provided. No notes or electronic devices. Optional scheduled breaks available between blocks (break time deducted from total). Step 3 Day 2 includes Computer-based Case Simulations (CCS) using patient management software.
renewal_options: USMLE scores do not expire for licensure purposes, though some state medical boards and residency programs may have time limits on Step scores.

prerequisites.description: Must be enrolled in or have graduated from an LCME- or AOA-accredited US/Canadian medical school (or equivalent ECFMG-eligible international medical school)

retake_policy.notes: Must wait at least 60 days before retaking the same Step exam. Maximum 3 attempts per 12-month period. Maximum 6 total attempts per Step. Full fee required for each retake.

official_study_resources.title: NBME Self-Assessments
official_study_resources.notes: Official predictive self-assessments for each Step; highly recommended
